hypothesis: Rounds 92 through 94 suggest the soft-codebook coarse branch is the first recent non-mainline idea with real signal, but the cheaper single-stage selectors (`lowrank_soft_codebook_residual`, `lowrank_grouped_soft_codebook_residual`, `lowrank_gated_soft_codebook_residual`) still did not beat the `lowrank_two_stage_residual` K128 anchor. The single most informative remaining matched architecture probe is `lowrank_asymmetric_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual`: it keeps the same LUTurbo-compatible static-library export path, but asks whether a front-loaded sparse budget after codebook projection can preserve most of the quality while shifting more of the work into the earlier coarse correction and reducing reliance on a large second-stage cleanup. This round answers one question only: under the exact matched `K=128`, `OPTIMIZER=signum`, `LR=2.0e-3`, `EXPANSION_FACTOR=12` recipe, is that asymmetric soft-codebook two-stage architecture intrinsically worth any future follow-up, or should the soft-codebook branch be treated as unable to justify more complexity than the current mainline?
summary: Run one matched architecture probe by switching only `ARCHITECTURE` to `lowrank_asymmetric_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual` while keeping `K=128`, `OPTIMIZER=signum`, `LR=2.0e-3`, and `EXPANSION_FACTOR=12` fixed.
self_review: This stays within the round policy: one probe, one primary variable, allowed `K`, and `EXPANSION_FACTOR=12` is explicit. I am choosing the asymmetric soft-codebook two-stage variant over routed, whitened, or bucketed alternatives because it is the simplest remaining probe that still targets LUTurbo-relevant execution structure rather than adding extra selector or preprocessing machinery. The main risk is that it still inherits more moving parts than the mainline, so only a clearly strong curve would justify keeping this branch alive.
notes_to_memory: Round 95 spends the single allowed matched architecture probe on `lowrank_asymmetric_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual` against the exact `lowrank_two_stage_residual` K128/EF12/signum/LR=2.0e-3 anchor.

Interpret this strictly as a family-worthiness test for whether the promising soft-codebook branch becomes more deployment-relevant when the sparse budget is front-loaded into the first refinement pass rather than split more evenly across two stages.
Return to the `lowrank_two_stage_residual` mainline next round regardless of outcome; do not open another new family immediately after this probe.
next_hypotheses: If `lowrank_asymmetric_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual` is clearly weaker than the matched `lowrank_two_stage_residual` anchor and not better than the earlier soft-codebook probes, close the asymmetric soft-codebook branch and resume mainline tuning next round.

If `lowrank_asymmetric_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual` stays surprisingly close, record it as a reserve branch with a more deployment-friendly stage allocation, but still return to `lowrank_two_stage_residual` next round per probe policy.
